title: coronilla minima subsp. clusii
intro: Coronilla minima subsp. clusii, also known as Clusius's coronilla, is a subspecies of the Coronilla minima plant, a low-growing perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Coronilla minima subsp. clusii include its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
